steps.title: Identify the most relevant information from the notes for the goal
steps.content:
The student wants to specify how long Despina's orbit takes.

The directly relevant information from the notes is: “Despina completes an orbit of Neptune in 0.33 Earth days on average.” The notes also mention Kepler's laws, but these provide background context rather than information needed to specify the duration.

steps.title: Check which choice uses that relevant information most effectively
steps.content:
Choice A directly states that Despina “completes its orbit of the planet in 0.33 Earth days on average”—using the exact relevant information to accomplish the goal efficiently.

The other choices either omit the duration, state it incorrectly, or bury it in unnecessary background.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
